title: Scorzoneroides helvetica
taxonomy: Scorzoneroides helvetica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Scorzoneroides and is a perennial plant. The plant is native to Europe and is often found in dry grasslands and other open habitats. Scorzoneroides helvetica is also known as the Alpine Crow-leaved Scabious or the Alpine Crow-leaved Scabious. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationships with other similar species.
uses: Scorzoneroides helvetica is a plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. It contains compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, due to its carminative and anti-spasmodic effects.
